reference_block.name: ai-diligence-and-assurance-statement
reference_block.title: AI diligence and assurance statement
reference_block.statement: Product Assistant Agent

reference_block.name: purpose
reference_block.title: Purpose
reference_block.statement: The Product Assistant Agent is an internal workplace support tool.
It helps UK Government digital teams apply product, service, delivery, accessibility and governance practice more consistently.
It supports thinking and structured work.
It does not replace professional judgement, decision‑making, or accountability.

reference_block.name: how-the-agent-was-created
reference_block.title: How the agent was created
reference_block.statement: The agent was created using Microsoft 365 Copilot Agent Builder as part of a Home Office Copilot Agents micro‑experiment.
Agent Builder is used as the hosting and execution environment only.
The agent’s behaviour, modes and guardrails are defined in external Markdown rulesets stored in SharePoint.
These files are treated as binding by the agent and sit outside the single instruction window of Agent Builder.
The design deliberately separates:
This structure was chosen to reduce drift, false confidence and inconsistent advice.

reference_block.name: use-of-ai-during-development
reference_block.title: Use of AI during development
reference_block.statement: Microsoft Copilot was used as a development assistant, not as an autonomous designer.
Copilot supported:

checklist.entry: synthesising publicly available guidance from GOV.UK, GDS and SFIA

reference_block.statement: All outputs were reviewed, edited and structured by the agent author before adoption.
No rules or constraints were accepted without human review.

reference_block.name: guardrails-and-risk-controls
reference_block.title: Guardrails and risk controls
reference_block.statement: The agent operates under a strict rule‑precedence model.
Law, safety, accessibility, privacy and governance override all other behaviour.
The most protective rule always applies.
The agent:

checklist.entry: refuses requests that bypass governance or service‑phase controls
avoids legal, operational or approval‑level decisions

reference_block.statement: Behavioural linting is used to block non‑compliant outputs, including inappropriate artefacts and incorrect mode usage.

reference_block.name: data-and-information-handling
reference_block.title: Data and information handling
reference_block.statement: The agent does not train on Home Office data.
It can only access information explicitly provided through configured knowledge sources, such as approved SharePoint locations.
The agent is intended for use with information classified up to OFFICIAL and follows Home Office guidance on safe use of Copilot.

reference_block.name: testing-and-oversight
reference_block.title: Testing and oversight
reference_block.statement: The agent has been tested through:

checklist.entry: shared testing with a small group of invited colleagues
walkthroughs and critique during Agent Builder support sessions

reference_block.statement: Feedback has been used to refine rules, clarify limits and tighten guardrails. The agent is experimental and not positioned as a production system.

reference_block.name: purpose-limitation-and-proportionality
reference_block.title: Purpose limitation and proportionality
reference_block.statement: The agent is a decision‑support tool.
It augments human judgement and operates within a clearly defined remit.
It does not act autonomously or replace accountability.

reference_block.name: human-oversight-and-accountability
reference_block.title: Human oversight and accountability
reference_block.statement: A named owner designed, configured and tested the agent.
All rules, modes and constraints were authored and reviewed by a human.
The agent:

checklist.entry: makes its active mode and phase visible
defers to users where judgement or escalation is required

reference_block.name: transparency-and-auditability
reference_block.title: Transparency and auditability
reference_block.statement: The agent’s behaviour is defined in human‑readable Markdown files.
These are reviewable and auditable.
Limits, refusal conditions and escalation paths are explicit.

reference_block.name: service-standard-1-understand-users-and-their-needs
reference_block.title: Service Standard 1: Understand users and their needs
reference_block.statement: The agent was designed around observed needs in delivery teams, including:
It supports users by structuring thinking, not by replacing it.

reference_block.name: service-standard-2-solve-a-whole-problem
reference_block.title: Service Standard 2: Solve a whole problem
reference_block.statement: The agent defaults to a multidisciplinary team mode.
It considers product, service, delivery, accessibility, security and governance together.
This avoids single‑discipline optimisation and supports joined‑up decision‑making.

reference_block.name: service-standard-4-make-the-service-simple-to-use
reference_block.title: Service Standard 4: Make the service simple to use
reference_block.statement: The agent:

checklist.entry: exposes clear commands for modes and phases

reference_block.statement: Complexity is handled in rules, not pushed onto users.

reference_block.name: service-standard-5-make-sure-everyone-can-use-the-service
reference_block.title: Service Standard 5: Make sure everyone can use the service
reference_block.statement: Accessibility is non‑negotiable.
The agent:

checklist.entry: refuses outputs that breach accessibility requirements

reference_block.statement: Accessibility is treated as a legal and quality requirement.

reference_block.name: service-standard-9-create-a-multidisciplinary-team
reference_block.title: Service Standard 9: Create a multidisciplinary team
reference_block.statement: The agent is explicitly modelled as a multidisciplinary team.
Specialist modes exist, but they sit within a shared reasoning framework.
This reinforces good practice rather than encouraging siloed working.

reference_block.name: service-standard-10-be-ethical-and-transparent
reference_block.title: Service Standard 10: Be ethical and transparent
reference_block.statement: The agent:
Users are expected to validate outputs and apply judgement.

reference_block.name: use-ai-lawfully-fairly-and-responsibly
reference_block.title: Use AI lawfully, fairly and responsibly
reference_block.statement: The agent:

checklist.entry: operates within UK law and policy
prioritises harm prevention and safety over usefulness

reference_block.statement: Rules explicitly prevent legal or operational decision‑making.

reference_block.name: be-clear-about-the-role-of-ai
reference_block.title: Be clear about the role of AI
reference_block.statement: The Product Assistant Agent is positioned as:
It is not positioned as authoritative, predictive, or autonomous.

reference_block.name: manage-risk-across-the-lifecycle
reference_block.title: Manage risk across the lifecycle
reference_block.statement: Risk controls are embedded at design time through:
The agent was iteratively tested and refined during the Copilot Agent Builder micro‑experiment, with feedback used to tighten guardrails.

reference_block.name: enable-scrutiny-and-challenge
reference_block.title: Enable scrutiny and challenge
reference_block.statement: Markdown‑based rules make behaviour visible and challengeable.
Because the rules are written in Markdown:
This supports scrutiny by delivery, assurance, and governance colleagues.

reference_block.name: statement-of-limitations
reference_block.title: Statement of limitations
reference_block.statement: The Product Assistant Agent:
Responsibility for decisions remains with humans at all times.

reference_block.name: status
reference_block.title: Status
reference_block.statement: This is an experimental, non‑production tool.
It is intended to support learning, consistency and good practice.
It should be used with appropriate care and oversight.
